**Trajectory assessment:** Friday 2026-05-08 was a **review-decision intake day plus three-stall day plus cross-project escalation day**. The dominant operational signal was the three-stall surface (largest single-day stall surface yet observed: 1pm register cleanup at "let's do it" prompt, sewing-agent-weekly hit org-limit, Wright/Rohr Sunday agent hit org-limit) combined with the explorer-fix Cowork session's two same-session org-limit interrupts. The dominant architectural signal was the C2A2 explorer Codex 5.5 external-review intake and queued-but-undelivered composite synthesis — the first observed external-LLM-review composition event in the project's history. Today's 12-to-8 presumption-to-assumption ratio (1.5:1) ties 2026-04-27's high, with the new clusters (ORG-LIMIT + COMPOSITE-SYNTHESIS, STALLED-TASK-CLOSURE, SANDBOX-INFRASTRUCTURE-LAYER, UNDIFFERENTIATED-DEFERRED-TREATMENT) signalling that the operational-process layer is generating more invisible dependencies than stated commitments. **If any one action moves the pipeline forward most**, it is performing a per-task link-count audit on the wiki-orchestrator daily task and the c2a2-self-awareness-daily task — both showing regression evidence consistent with ASSUMPTION-092. If `stat -c %h` returns 1 on either, the `update_scheduled_task --fireAt` workaround (ASSUMPTION-081) can be retriggered immediately and the regression hypothesis is supported. **If a second**, it is shipping the two-line `extractOverview()` fix in `wiki_narration.html` (ASSUMPTION-090) and adding a smoke-test script per Codex 5.5's recommendation — this stops Chrome from logging an uncaught exception on every explorer page-load. **If a third**, it is filing the combined sandbox-infrastructure escalation note to Anthropic per ASSUMPTION-094 (5 channels across 2 projects justifies single combined report); PRESUMPTION-110's same-layer presumption is the open question for that escalation. **And if a fourth**, it is building a fallback delivery channel for the cowork-to-chat sync (Gmail self-thread, registered queue file, etc.) per PRESUMPTION-111 — three consecutive failed deliveries with no fallback design is the longest unaddressed channel-degradation in the project's history.
